questions.question: A student uses a magnifying glass to focus the Sun's rays onto a piece of paper. A small bright spot is formed when the lens is $12\text{ cm}$ from the paper. What is the focal length of the lens?
options: $12\text{ cm}$
$6\text{ cm}$
$24\text{ cm}$
It cannot be determined without the Sun's distance
questions.explanation:
Because the Sun is a distant object, its rays are parallel. These rays focus at the focal point, so the distance from the lens to the bright spot is the focal length.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: If a parallel beam of light is incident on a thin converging lens at an angle (not parallel to the principal axis), where will the rays meet?
options: At a point on the focal plane
At the principal focus $F$
At the optical centre $C$
They will never meet because they diverge
questions.explanation:
Rays that are parallel to each other but not to the principal axis are refracted to a point on the focal plane, rather than the specific principal focus $F$.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: Lens X and Lens Y are both made of the same glass. Lens X has more curved surfaces than Lens Y. Which statement is correct?
options: Lens X has a shorter focal length than Lens Y
Lens Y has a shorter focal length than Lens X
Both lenses have the same focal length
Lens X is a diverging lens and Lens Y is a converging lens
questions.explanation:
Increased curvature of a lens surface causes individual rays to refract by larger angles, which brings them to a focus closer to the lens (shorter focal length).

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: What is the primary action of a thin diverging lens on a parallel beam of light?
options: It causes light rays to spread out as if they came from a point
It causes light rays to converge to a single point
It reflects the light rays back towards the source
It has no effect on the direction of parallel rays
questions.explanation:
A diverging lens is thinner in the centre and causes parallel light rays to diverge (spread out) from a point.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: How many focal points (principal focuses) does a single thin lens have?
options: Two
One
Four
Zero
questions.explanation:
A lens has two focal points, one located on each side of the lens at an equal distance from the optical centre.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.
